quick_facts.label: Recruitment channel
quick_facts.value: The post has been recruited under Railway Recruitment Boards’ paramedical notifications; CEN 04/2024 listed Health & Malaria Inspector Grade III among the paramedical categories.

quick_facts.label: Pay level and medical standard
quick_facts.value: In the 2024 RRB paramedical notice, the post was listed at 7th CPC Pay Level 6, with an initial basic pay of ₹35,400 and medical standard C1. This is dated recruitment-notice context, not a guarantee of pay or conditions in a future notification.

quick_facts.label: Educational route
quick_facts.value: The Indian Railway Establishment Manual specifies a B.Sc. with Chemistry plus either a one-year Diploma of Health/Sanitary Inspector or a one-year National Trade Certificate in Health Sanitary Inspector awarded by NCVT for direct recruitment.

quick_facts.label: Most recently verified national vacancy context
quick_facts.value: CEN 04/2024 advertised 126 Health & Malaria Inspector Grade III vacancies across all RRBs, with applications open from August 17, 2024 to September 16, 2024. Those dates and vacancy numbers belong to that completed cycle and should not be treated as current openings.

quick_facts.label: Historical promotion channel
quick_facts.value: The Establishment Manual records Health & Malaria Inspector Grade II and Grade I as the normal higher grades for this cadre; actual promotional opportunities depend on the applicable railway rules, vacancies, seniority, and selection procedures.

sections.heading: Public-health and vector-control role
paragraphs: Health & Malaria Inspector Grade III is part of the Railway medical and sanitary public-health function. Railway medical-manual material places the inspector in the anti-malaria supply-and-supervision chain: malaria mates receive anti-malaria stores from the Health & Malaria Inspector and maintain accounts for them.

The associated operational work in Railway colonies and service premises includes approved insecticide spraying, destruction of mosquito larvae at breeding sites, drainage or treatment of stagnant water, and removal of vegetation around potential breeding locations. This indicates a role centered on organizing and inspecting sanitation and vector-control activity, maintaining accountability for materials, and coordinating subordinate field staff rather than only desk-based hospital work.
bullets: Track sanitation and vector-control work through field inspection, supplies, records, and coordination.
Support mosquito-control measures in Railway colonies and service buildings, including work directed at breeding sites and stagnant water.
Work within Railway Medical Department arrangements alongside medical officers and sanitary/anti-malarial staff.

sections.heading: Entry through RRB paramedical recruitment
paragraphs: The principal open-market route is an RRB paramedical recruitment notification when the post is included. The latest verified national notice in the available official record is CEN 04/2024; it listed Health & Malaria Inspector Grade III as a Level 6 paramedical post. Its application period ended on September 16, 2024, so applicants should rely on a newly issued RRB notice for any future vacancy, eligibility date, age limit, examination process, fee, and medical requirements.

The Railway Establishment Manual sets the core direct-recruitment education combination as a B.Sc. with Chemistry plus a one-year Health/Sanitary Inspector diploma or the specified one-year NCVT Health Sanitary Inspector trade certificate. Railway Board clarification published by South Central Railway also shows that not every similarly named sanitation diploma is automatically accepted; candidates should match their qualification exactly against the notification and its equivalence instructions.
bullets: Complete a B.Sc. in which Chemistry meets the notified requirement.
Obtain the required one-year Health/Sanitary Inspector qualification or specified NCVT certificate.
Apply only when an RRB paramedical CEN includes this post, then meet the notification’s eligibility, identity, document-verification, and C1 medical-standard requirements.

sections.heading: Skills developed and used
paragraphs: The specified academic route makes applied chemistry and sanitary-health knowledge foundational. The field activity described in Railway medical-manual material also points to practical competence in environmental sanitation, mosquito breeding-source control, safe handling and accounting of anti-malaria supplies, observation during inspections, and clear reporting to the medical administration.

Because work is delivered through a chain that includes malaria mates and anti-malaria field staff, useful professional skills include task coordination, record keeping, communication of hygiene instructions, and careful follow-through on corrective action.
bullets: Chemistry-informed understanding of sanitation and anti-malarial materials.
Inspection observation, registers and supply accountability.
Field-team coordination and communication with Railway medical personnel.

sections.heading: Work setting
paragraphs: This is a Railway Medical Department public-health post with work connected to Railway colonies, service buildings, dispensary or medical-administration arrangements, and other Railway premises where sanitation and vector-control measures are organized. The underlying operational material specifically describes anti-malaria activity in Railway colonies and service buildings, including drainage and breeding-site control.

The C1 medical standard shown in CEN 04/2024 is a recruitment medical-fitness requirement for that cycle. Applicants should check the medical standards and job-location details in the specific RRB notice they apply under.
bullets: A mix of field inspection and administrative record/supply work.
Coordination with medical officers and sanitary or anti-malarial personnel.
Potential postings vary by the Railway zone, division, and vacancy allocation in the recruitment notice.

sections.heading: Career progression context
paragraphs: The Indian Railway Establishment Manual identifies Health & Malaria Inspector Grade II and Health & Malaria Inspector Grade I as the normal higher grades after Grade III. The same manual historically describes a mix of direct recruitment through RRBs and selection-based promotion of qualified serving Medical Department employees into Grade III.

These manual provisions provide a useful cadre map, but an individual’s promotion timing, designation, pay level, selection procedure, and training requirements are governed by the rules and vacancy position in force in the relevant railway administration at that time.
bullets: Entry cadre: Health & Malaria Inspector Grade III.
Historically listed higher grades: Health & Malaria Inspector Grade II, then Grade I.
Progression is contingent on service rules, eligibility, selection, and available posts.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: For the 2024 RRB paramedical recruitment cycle, Health & Malaria Inspector Grade III was advertised at 7th CPC Pay Level 6 with an initial basic pay of ₹35,400. Actual take-home pay varies with admissible allowances, place of posting, deductions, and future government pay decisions; the 2024 figure should therefore be used as historical recruitment context rather than a current salary quote.
The 2024 notice also listed 126 all-RRB vacancies for the post. Vacancy counts, zone-wise allocation, eligibility dates, and recruitment timelines change between notifications.
bullets: 2024 notification context: Pay Level 6; initial basic pay ₹35,400.
Allowances and deductions are variable and posting-dependent.
Always use the active RRB CEN for current compensation and vacancy information.
